instruction: Extract all core computer science concepts from the following lecture JSON. Return only a newline-separated list of unique concepts. Do not explain anything.

input.title: Bringing back our alphabet soup: The C ABI

text_blocks.text: Bringing back our alphabet soup: The C ABI

runs.text: Bringing back our alphabet soup: The C ABI

text_blocks.text: The C Application Binary Interface (ABI) are assembly conventions
Like MIPS, certain registers are typically used for returns values, args, etc

runs.text: Like MIPS, certain registers are typically used for returns values,

text_blocks.text: It is not defined by the language, but rather the OS.

runs.text: It is not defined by the language, but rather the OS.

text_blocks.text: Windows and Linux (UNIX/System V) have a different C ABI 

runs.text: Windows and Linux (UNIX/System V) have a different C ABI

text_blocks.text: In our x86-64 Linux C ABI, registers are used to pass arguments:

runs.text: In our x86-64 Linux C ABI, registers are used to pass arguments:

text_blocks.text: Remaining arguments go on the stack.

runs.text: Remaining arguments go on the stack.

text_blocks.text: Callee must preserve %rbp, %rbx, %r12, %r13, %r14, %r15 (Like MIPS s0 – s7)
Return value: %rax (overflows into %rdx for 128-bits) (MIPS v0 – v1)
Lots of other small things not worth going over.

runs.text: Lots of other small things not worth going over.

text_blocks.text: The activation frame contains temporary data needed by the function.

runs.text: contains temporary data needed by the function.

text_blocks.text: %rbp is the address of this frame

runs.text: is the address of this frame

text_blocks.text: 1124:       c7 45 fc fe ff ff ff    movl   $0xfffffffe,-0x4(%rbp)
First: it fills the activation framewith initial variable values.
It may not allocate them inany strict order. Here, itallocates x first and further away.

runs.text: It may not allocate them in

text_blocks.text: 1124:       c7 45 fc fe ff ff ff    movl   $0xfffffffe,-0x4(%rbp)
(it does not have to be in order)

runs.text: (it does not have to be in order)

When identifying functions, you are looking for thattell-tale sandwich pattern.

text_blocks.text: A push is a good sign of the beginning of a function

runs.text: is a good sign of the beginning of a function

text_blocks.text: And the pop will happen before the ret at the end.
Everything between is the sweet, sweet jam that makes it unique.

input.title: Who controls the cmp controls the flow

text_blocks.text: Who controls the cmp controls the flow
Control flow is a cmp or test followed by j*
cmp will set FLAGS based on the difference (subtraction) between values

runs.text: based on the difference (subtraction) between values

text_blocks.text: test will set FLAGS based on bitwise AND of both values (faster, but less useful)

runs.text: based on bitwise AND of both values (faster, but less useful)

text_blocks.text: j* group set %rip (program counter) to an address based on FLAGS

runs.text: (program counter) to an address based on

text_blocks.text: Often it is much more useful to just interpret the jmp ( jge is >= )

runs.text: Often it is much more useful to just interpret the

input.title: Who controls the cmp controls the flow

text_blocks.text: Who controls the cmp controls the flow
FLAGS has bits that are set based on the ALU (CPU math logic) result

runs.text: has bits that are set based on the ALU (CPU math logic) result

text_blocks.text: SF – most significant bit of result
Each jump looks at different FLAGS patterns. (Look ‘em up!)
Jump if the result (that is, x)does not have a set sign bit.

runs.text: Jump if the result (that is, x)
does not have a set sign bit.

text_blocks.text: Jump if the result is 0 orif result is negative after overflowor positive and didn’t overflow.

runs.text: Jump if the result is 0 or
if result is negative after overflow

text_blocks.text: ZF – set if result is zero
CF – set if last bit operation has carry

runs.text: – set if last bit operation has carry

text_blocks.text: jge – set if SF = OF or ZF = 1
(thus, instead of its strict definition,better to think about it abstractly)

runs.text: better to think about it abstractly)
